settings.title: How to Make Morning Goodbyes Easier
settings.excerpt: Tearful drop-offs worry every parent, so here is what actually helps a child settle more quickly.
settings.body:
Almost every family goes through a patch of difficult goodbyes, and it is one of the questions we hear most. The good news is that a few small habits make a real difference.

Keep it short. A long, drawn-out farewell tends to make the worry bigger, not smaller. A warm hug and a clear goodbye work best.

Always say goodbye. Slipping away unseen can shake a child's trust, even when it feels kinder in the moment.

Most children settle within minutes of a parent leaving. If yours is having a harder week, talk to their key worker. Settling in is something we do together.
